title: Ganua palembanica
intro: Ganua palembanica is a species of plant in the family Loganiaceae, commonly known as a medicinal herb. The health benefits of Ganua palembanica include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as fever and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ganua palembanica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy:

It is a species of flowering plant, which is native to Indonesia.

The classification of Ganua palembanica is not extensively documented in widely available sources.
